audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for gospel tradition, resurrection witnesses, if Messiah not raised, firstfruits, Adam/Messiah, resurrection body, mystery, death swallowed, steadfast labor.

verses.restored: Now I make known to you, siblings, the good news that I announced to you, which you also received, in which you also stand,
through which also you are being saved/healed, if you hold fast the word I announced to you—unless you trusted in vain.
For I handed over to you among first things what I also received: that Messiah died for our sins/misalignments according to the Scriptures,
and that he was buried, and that he has been raised on the third day according to the Scriptures,
and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
Then he appeared to more than five hundred siblings at once, most of whom remain until now, but some have slept.
Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
Last of all, as to one untimely born, he appeared also to me.
For I am the least of the apostles, not fit to be called apostle, because I pursued/persecuted the assembly of the Divine.
But by grace of the Divine I am what I am, and his grace toward me did not become empty; rather I labored more abundantly than all of them, yet not I, but the grace of the Divine with me.
Therefore whether I or they, so we proclaim and so you trusted.
Now if Messiah is proclaimed that he has been raised from the dead, how do some among you say there is no resurrection of dead ones?
But if there is no resurrection of dead ones, neither has Messiah been raised.
And if Messiah has not been raised, then our proclamation is empty, and your trust also empty.
And we are found false witnesses of the Divine, because we witnessed against the Divine that he raised Messiah, whom he did not raise if indeed dead ones are not raised.
For if dead ones are not raised, neither has Messiah been raised.
And if Messiah has not been raised, your trust is futile; you are still in your sins/misalignments.
Then also those who slept in Messiah perished.
If in Messiah we have hoped in this life only, we are more pitiable than all humans.
But now Messiah has been raised from the dead, firstfruit of those who have slept.
For since through a human came death, through a human also came resurrection of dead ones.
For just as in Adam all die, so also in Messiah all will be made alive.
But each in one's own order: Messiah firstfruit, then those of Messiah at his arrival/presence,
then the completion, when he hands over the reign to the Divine and Father, when he makes inactive every ruler and every authority and power.
For it is necessary for him to reign until he places all enemies under his feet.
The last enemy being made inactive is death.
For he subjected all things under his feet. But when it says all things have been subjected, it is clear that the One who subjected all things to him is excepted.
When all things are subjected to him, then the Son himself also will be subjected to the One who subjected all things to him, so that the Divine may be all in all.
Otherwise, what will those do who are baptized for the dead? If dead ones are not raised at all, why are they baptized for them?
Why also are we in danger every hour?
I die daily, by the boasting in you, siblings, which I have in Messiah Jesus our Lord.
If according to human reasoning I fought beasts in Ephesus, what is the profit to me? If dead ones are not raised, Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.
Do not be deceived: Harmful companionships corrupt useful/good character.
Sober up righteously/justly and do not sin/misalign; for some have ignorance of the Divine. I speak this toward your shame.
But someone will say, How are dead ones raised? With what sort of body do they come?
Foolish one, what you sow is not made alive unless it dies.
And what you sow, you do not sow the body that will come to be, but a bare grain, perhaps of wheat or of some other.
But the Divine gives it a body just as he willed, and to each of the seeds its own body.
Not all flesh is the same flesh, but one of humans, another flesh of animals, another flesh of birds, another of fish.
And there are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies; but different is the glory of the heavenly, and different of the earthly.
One glory of sun, another glory of moon, another glory of stars; for star differs from star in glory.
So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in decay, it is raised in incorruptibility.
It is sown in dishonor, it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness, it is raised in power.
It is sown a natural/soul-level body, it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural/soul-level body, there is also a spiritual body.
So also it is written: The first human, Adam, became a living soul; the last Adam became life-making Spirit.
But the spiritual is not first, but the natural/soul-level; afterward the spiritual.
The first human is from earth, made of dust; the second human is from heaven.
As the one made of dust, so also those made of dust; and as the heavenly one, so also the heavenly ones.
And just as we bore the image of the one made of dust, we will also bear the image of the heavenly one.
This I say, siblings, that flesh and blood are not able to inherit the reign of the Divine, nor does decay inherit incorruptibility.
Look, I tell you a mystery: we will not all sleep, but we will all be changed,
in an instant, in a blink of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised incorruptible, and we will be changed.
For this decaying one must put on incorruptibility, and this dying one put on deathlessness.
When this decaying one puts on incorruptibility and this dying one puts on deathlessness, then the word written will come to be: Death was swallowed into victory.
Where, O death, is your victory? Where, O death, is your sting?
The sting of death is sin/misalignment, and the power of sin/misalignment is the Law.
But thanks to the Divine, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Messiah.
Therefore, my beloved siblings, become steadfast, immovable, abounding in the work of the Lord always, knowing that your labor is not empty in the Lord.

translation_notes: Spiritual body does not mean non-body; it is body animated/transformed by Spirit. Paul is anti-decay, not anti-body.
Firstfruits and resurrection language should preserve embodied/cosmic hope rather than escape from creation.
